what does `tenex-edge who` list? what's in the output itself?

Explained what `tenex-edge who` outputs: markdown tables for agents when piped, colorized terminal display for humans, with fields for agent name, host, title, and status, and clarified that the wiki was outdated about the code being dead.

I want to completely refactor its output, and when ran by a human we would just show the full list of channels. We also need to allow them to express hierarchy when referring to a channel like when switching. This is a brainstorming session, don't take my proposal too literally, run some opus agents to brainstorm and propose some outputs.

Ran four brainstorming agents to evaluate the design proposal and synthesized their feedback, identifying where they converged (slash-path hierarchy, compact scoped views, full paths for agents, disambiguation approach) vs where they disagreed (bundled vs split commands, identifier format, invite semantics, roster freshness).

A: one bundle -- agents are not supposed to use `who` anyway actively -- it's mostly a way to get a complete state snapshot, but we are injecting the exact same data as delta via hooks (we need to also make sure we don't deviate from that format -- so do send an agent to research that and fold that into the plan -- we want ONE format, it just happens to be that the hook-part also leverages deltas to show what changed within the context of the stuff the agent we're injecting stuff to cares about (i.e. we don't show the full list of channels to the agent via hooks) B: slug@backend -- this is how they will mention remote agents for example `tenex-edge chat write "@developer hello"` means the developer in the local computer, which is a different agent than `tenex-edge chat write "@developer@remote hello there"` which is a remote agent that just happens to have the same slug C: explicit invite, not auto-add D: no, only on deltas (i.e. when a new agent was added to the roster) Tell me what you propose based on all this

Proposed a unified FabricView model rendering to both who output and hook deltas with the same row grammar, fixing current bugs (opaque-id leaks, mismatched identifiers, divergent status rendering) and implementing the decisions (project-scoped paths, agent-focused identifiers, explicit invite, roster-delta injection).

6: humans don't use channels switch -- that's only for agents your first example looks wrong: channels are always scoped to the current project, if the project is "tenex-edge" then swithich to the tenex-edge/planning channel would just be "planning" (otherwise everything would require the tenex-edge prefix like tenex-edge/epic999/planning) if agents want to indicate a channel of a different project maybe we can give them something like tenex-edge channel read --project some-other-project epic2344/research -- (switching channels to a different project is probably a bad idea, or at least not necessary now)

Acknowledged the corrections (channels switch is agent-only, paths are project-scoped without prefix), updated the disambiguation grammar to drop cross-project absolute paths, and confirmed the unified implementation plan.

Implemented all six features (channel-label opaque-id leak fix, remote-aware identifiers, channels switch resolver, invite command, agents roster, roster-change delta) with 277 lib tests passing and full build clean.
